values.value:
-- APEX BEST PRACTICES (enforced — non-compliant code will fail code review) -- 1. NEVER write SOQL queries inside for loops. WRONG: for (Invoice__c inv : invList) { Account a = [SELECT Id FROM Account WHERE Id = :inv.AccountId__c]; } CORRECT: Map<Id, Account> accMap = new Map<Id, Account>([SELECT Id FROM Account WHERE Id IN :accountIds]); 2. NEVER use DML inside loops. 3. Collect IDs/fields in a Set or List first, then query in bulk outside the loop. 4. All trigger handler methods MUST accept the full list/map (List<SObject>, Map<Id,SObject>). 5. Avoid hardcoded IDs — use dynamic queries or Custom Labels/Custom Metadata instead. 6. All Apex classes must be api version 61.0. 7. Use System.TriggerOperation enum for switch logic — never use Trigger.isBefore/isInsert booleans directly in the handler. 8. Always use correct API names from orgState.existingFields — never guess or invent field names. 9. Use Date.today() to get the current date — NEVER Date.now() (it does not exist on the Date class). 10. To check if a String field is blank in Apex, use String.isBlank(field). WRONG: if (l.Description == null || l.Description == '') CORRECT: if (String.isBlank(l.Description)) 11. NEVER use String.matches() — this method does NOT exist in Apex. CORRECT: Pattern p = Pattern.compile('^[+]?[0-9]{10,15}$'); Matcher m = p.matcher(phone); if (m.matches()) { 12. ALWAYS prefix sObject field references with the loop variable — never use bare field names. WRONG: if (AI_Confidence_Score__c < 0) CORRECT: if (record.AI_Confidence_Score__c < 0) 13. NEVER redefine or re-create fields that already exist in the org. 14. NEVER derive or guess field API names from ticket text — ALWAYS look up from orgState.existingFields. 15. NEVER derive or guess the sObject API name from the ticket label — ALWAYS use orgState.existingObjects. - NEVER use \+ in Apex regex. Use [+] instead. CORRECT: Pattern.compile('^[+]?[0-9]{10,15}$') Avoid ALL backslash sequences in regex where a character class alternative exists: \d → [0-9], \+ → [+], \. → [.]
-- APEX VALIDATION RULES (CRITICAL) -- 1. NEVER silently replace invalid field values with defaults. Validation means rejecting bad data with addError(), NOT overwriting it. WRONG: record.Content__c = 'Default content'; CORRECT: record.addError('Content cannot be empty.'); 2. NEVER leave stub methods that return hardcoded values. WRONG: private static Boolean isValidPhone(String phone) { return true; } CORRECT: implement the full Pattern.compile() + Matcher implementation 3. ALWAYS add a null check before comparing a Number field. WRONG: if (record.AI_Confidence_Score__c < 0) CORRECT: if (record.AI_Confidence_Score__c != null && record.AI_Confidence_Score__c < 0) 4. NEVER use hardcoded magic values. Use named constants: private static final Integer MIN_SCORE = 0; 5. handleBeforeUpdate MUST re-run all the same validations as handleBeforeInsert. 6. NEVER leave TODO comments or placeholder comments in generated code. Generated code must be complete and production-ready. - NEVER use Pattern.matches(regex, input) — does not exist in Apex. CORRECT: Pattern p = Pattern.compile('^[+]?[0-9]{10,15}$'); Matcher m = p.matcher(record.Client_Phone_No__c); if (!m.matches()) { record.addError('...'); } - NEVER generate CustomField XML for any field that already exists in orgState.existingFields. - BEFORE writing any CustomObject XML, scan orgState.existingFields for the object. If ANY field has type containing "MasterDetail" → sharingModel MUST be ControlledByParent.
Apex governor limits, bulkification, regex, validation, null checks best practices
